title: Breaking Down Innodata Inc. (INOD) Financial Health: Key Insights for Investors
text: INOD Financial Health Debt Cash Flow and Liquidity Position. Cash Position and Liquidity: Cash grew from $46.9 million at end of 2024 to $73.9 million by Q3 2025. Company remained debt-free and did not tap credit facility, demonstrating strong liquidity management. Excellent financial health with minimal debt, reflected in low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.14 and robust current ratio of 2.69. Strong cash positions with cash ratio of 1.66 and healthy cash flow coverage ratios, providing ample liquidity. Debt Position: Company essentially debt-free on balance sheet, huge advantage in rising-rate environment. INOD has $30 million untouched credit facility and minimal leverage with D/E ratio between 0.05 and 0.10. Cash Flow: Company funding significant expansion through strong operating cash flow and equity, not taking on new debt. Cash flow from financing activities small net inflow of about $1.14 million for quarter, suggesting minimal reliance on new debt or equity financing to sustain operations. Overall Financial Health: INOD demonstrates strong financial health with solid cash position, minimal debt obligations, and healthy liquidity ratios providing significant financial flexibility.
